metadata.title: CarrIA - Discover Your Professional Potential
metadata.description: We do not recommend popular careers, we recommend careers compatible with how you work. AI-based career mapping platform with gamification, simulations, and international networking.

welcome.heroTextStart: We do not recommend popular careers, we recommend careers
welcome.heroTextEnd: Resumes show the past. Our system shows your
welcome.profileExamples: Examples of profiles that may match you
welcome.startDescription: Fill in your details to personalize your experience
welcome.conditionsQuestion: Do you have any condition you would like to share?
welcome.dataUsage: Your data will only be used to personalize your results

features.aiDescription: Analysis of PROFILE, GOALS, and RHYTHMS

audiences.schoolsDescription: Vocational guidance + college recommendations aligned with the profile
audiences.companiesDescription: Candidate selection and career transition based on classification
audiences.youthDescription: Discover how to start professional life with clarity and direction
audiences.studentsDescription: Develop soft skills and prepare for the global market with exchange programs

decision.title: When you need to solve something important, do you usually act on impulse or think before deciding?
decision.placeholder: Tell us how you usually make decisions when something really matters.
decision.nexo: Let's understand how you make important decisions. This reveals a lot about your strategic profile!

startProjects.nexo: The way you start projects is a key characteristic of your professional profile.
startProjects.title: When you start something new, do you prefer to jump straight in or understand it better before starting?
startProjects.placeholder: How do you usually react when you're starting something completely new?

replan.nexo: Flexibility and planning go hand in hand in the modern professional world.
replan.title: If something goes wrong along the way, do you tend to fix it immediately or pause to reorganize everything?
replan.placeholder: Can you recall a time when something did not go as expected? What did you do?

preferences.nexo: Understanding your preferences is the first step to strengthening them!
preferences.title: Do you feel more comfortable when you know exactly what will happen, or when you can discover things gradually?
preferences.placeholder: Do you prefer to stay in control of the situation or adapt to what comes along?

followIdeas.nexo: Problem-solving is a highly valued skill in the market!
followIdeas.title: Do you prefer following ready-made ideas or creating your own ways of doing things?
followIdeas.placeholder: Do you usually follow examples or prefer to do things your own way?

taskStyle.nexo: The way you approach tasks says a lot about what kind of career will make you happy!
taskStyle.title: When someone gives you a task, do you prefer to follow instructions or add your own touch?
taskStyle.placeholder: When someone guides you, do you adapt or follow exactly as explained?

motivation.nexo: Motivation and creativity are essential skills!
motivation.title: Do you feel more motivated when everything is already defined or when you can explore possibilities?
motivation.placeholder: What motivates you more: a structured plan or the freedom to explore ideas?

focusMultitask.nexo: Balancing focus and multitasking is a constant challenge at work!
focusMultitask.title: Do you prefer finishing one thing at a time or handling multiple tasks at once?
focusMultitask.placeholder: Do you prefer focusing on one thing or splitting your attention across multiple tasks?

persistence.nexo: Your persistence says a lot about your professional style!
persistence.title: When you start something, do you usually stick with it despite difficulties or lose interest along the way?
persistence.placeholder: Think of something you started — what made you continue or stop?

workPace.nexo: How you handle your work pace says a lot about your professional maturity!
workPace.title: Do you feel more comfortable following a defined pace or creating your own rhythm?
workPace.placeholder: Do you prefer routine or freedom to work at your own pace?

communicationStyle.nexo: Emotional intelligence is essential for any successful career!
communicationStyle.title: Do you usually say what you think right away or prefer to observe before taking a position?
communicationStyle.placeholder: In conversations, do you tend to speak quickly or observe more before sharing your opinion?

leadershipInitiative.nexo: What you do when no one leads reflects the leader you can become!
leadershipInitiative.title: In a situation where no one makes a decision, do you tend to step forward or wait for someone else to take initiative?
leadershipInitiative.placeholder: When no one makes a decision, do you usually act or wait for someone else to start?

participationStyle.nexo: Final question! Your participation style is essential for your career!
participationStyle.title: Do you feel more comfortable being the center of interactions or participating in a more discreet way?
participationStyle.placeholder: In a group, do you tend to stand out or prefer to participate more quietly?

creativeProblemSolving.nexo: Time to explore your creativity! How do you approach problems and challenges?
creativeProblemSolving.title: When something does not work as expected, do you usually repeat what you already know or try something different?
creativeProblemSolving.placeholder: Have you ever solved something in a different way?

executionStart.nexo: Now let's see how you execute and deliver results!
executionStart.title: When you have several things to do, do you usually organize everything first or solve things as they come up?
executionStart.placeholder: How do you organize yourself when you have several tasks at the same time?

relationshipStart.nexo: Final section! Let's understand how you relate to other people at work.
relationshipStart.title: When you need to solve something difficult, do you prefer doing it alone or exchanging ideas with other people?
relationshipStart.placeholder: When a problem comes up, do you usually ask for opinions or solve it on your own?

reaction.intro: Great! Now I want to test your reaction speed. This helps me understand your decision-making style under pressure.

reactionTest.title: Reaction Test
reactionTest.instruction: Click quickly when the screen turns green
reactionTest.question: Now let's see your decision speed!

sortingValues.nexo: This one is very important! Your values guide all your career decisions. Show me what truly matters to you.
sortingValues.title: Values Ranking
sortingValues.question: Rank your career values by priority

instructions.order: Rank from most important to least important:

crossword.nexo: Final mini-game! Solve the crossword using professional world terms!
crossword.title: Crossword
crossword.description: Solve the crossword using careers and soft skills terms

labels.hintInfo: Click the lamp to reveal a letter. Using hints will affect your final ranking.

success.clue: Positive result after overcoming a challenge

core.clue: Central part of an idea or project

profileDescriptions.strategic: You have a mindset focused on planning and future vision.
profileDescriptions.creative: You stand out for your ability to create innovative solutions.
profileDescriptions.execution: You are focused on results and excellent delivery.
profileDescriptions.relational: You have a natural ability to connect with and influence people.

details.nameCreated: was created automatically based on your
details.traitScoresQuestion: What are my scores in each trait?
details.dreamCareerQuestion: How compatible am I with my dream career?
details.firstJobQuestion: How long until I get my first job in the field?
details.firstJobAnswer: The average transition time varies according to the chosen career and your current skill level. On average, with dedication and the right development plan, you can get your first opportunity in 6-12 months.
details.marketTrendsQuestion: What are the market trends for these careers?
details.marketTrendsAnswer: The recommended careers show above-average market growth. With accelerated digital transformation, professionals with technology, product, and design skills are in high demand globally.
details.softSkillsQuestion: How can I develop soft skills effectively?
details.softSkillsAnswer: Soft skills are developed through conscious practice and constant feedback. We recommend joining team projects, seeking mentorship, taking personal development courses, and practicing regular self-assessment.
details.potentialQuestion: What is my compatibility potential after developing these skills?
details.internationalQuestion: How does the international experience work?
details.internationalAnswer: International experiences include exchange programs, courses abroad, and global networking to accelerate your career development through unique cultural experiences. You can explore programs in several countries and fields.
details.networkingQuestion: How can I build international networking?
details.networkingAnswer: Connect with professionals worldwide through events, online communities, mentorship programs, and international experiences. Build a global network of relevant contacts for your career.
details.countriesQuestion: Which countries are recommended for studying?
details.scholarshipsAnswer: Yes! There are several scholarship options for international programs. Research government programs, university scholarships, and private foundations to find the best opportunities around the world.

m60.title: M60 - University of Exchange
m60.subtitle: Your partner for global career development
m60.description: M60 can help you achieve your dream career through international experiences, study abroad programs, and global networking. Develop your soft skills by experiencing different cultures!

memory.title: Memory Game
memory.description: Find the pairs to reveal your focus level
memory.question: Let's test your focus and concentration skills!
memory.aiMessage: Hi! I'm Nexo, your guide on this self-knowledge journey. Let's start with a quick game so I can understand how you work!
